notes: Confirmed: SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta property exists but only Windows MAUI passes real values (via MouseWheelDelta which is already v120). Android hardcodes wheelDelta=0, Apple/Tizen use the basic constructor (no wheelDelta param, defaults to 0), and Blazor/WPF/WinForms/GTK have no wheel event handling at all. The v120 normalization standard requested in this enhancement does not exist yet. Tested on both 3.119.0-preview.1.2 and 3.116.1 — same feature gap in both versions.

Verify Apple and Tizen handlers use basic constructor with no wheelDelta parameter (defaults to 0)

versionResults.notes: Feature gap confirmed: WheelDelta property exists but only Windows MAUI populates it. All other platforms default to 0 or have no wheel handling. Enhancement request — no bug to reproduce.
Same feature gap as preview version. WheelDelta defaults to 0 on non-Windows platforms in latest stable as well.

actionability.reason: Maintainer-filed tracking enhancement with seven sub-issues (#3534-#3540). Feature gap confirmed — WheelDelta is only populated on Windows MAUI. All other platforms hardcode 0 or have no wheel handling. Work is already decomposed and prioritized.

actions.description: Update area label to area/SkiaSharp.Views since all work is in platform view handlers

proposedResponse.body:
Reproduction confirms the feature gap described in this tracking issue.

**Findings:**
- `SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta` property exists (`int`, read-only)
- **Windows MAUI**: Only platform that passes real `MouseWheelDelta` values (already v120)
- **Android MAUI**: Hardcodes `wheelDelta=0` in all touch event constructors
- **Apple/Tizen MAUI**: Use the basic 4-param constructor which has no `wheelDelta` parameter (defaults to 0)
- **Blazor/WPF/WinForms/GTK**: No wheel or scroll event handling exists at all

Tested on both SkiaSharp 3.119.0-preview.1.2 and 3.116.1 — same gap in both versions. The v120 normalization standard does not exist yet. Work is tracked via sub-issues #3534–#3540.
